Reconnaissance.label: Confirm scope and rules of engagement
Reconnaissance.description: Verify authorized targets, exclusions, testing windows, credentials, and stop-test contacts before active work

Reconnaissance.label: Build target inventory
Reconnaissance.description: Normalize domains, IP ranges, ASNs, subsidiaries, cloud assets, and application URLs into a working target list

Reconnaissance.label: WHOIS and registrar lookup
Reconnaissance.description: Gather domain registration, registrar, nameserver, and ownership information

Reconnaissance.label: DNS enumeration
Reconnaissance.description: Enumerate DNS records including A, AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, CNAME, SPF, DKIM, and DMARC

Reconnaissance.label: Certificate transparency search
Reconnaissance.description: Search certificate transparency logs for related hostnames, wildcard certificates, and forgotten environments

Reconnaissance.label: Subdomain enumeration
Reconnaissance.description: Discover subdomains using passive and active sources such as amass, subfinder, crt.sh, and DNS brute force

Reconnaissance.label: OSINT - search engine dorking
Reconnaissance.description: Use Google/Bing dorks to find exposed pages, documents, admin panels, backups, and indexed secrets

Reconnaissance.label: Shodan / Censys search
Reconnaissance.description: Search internet-wide scan sources for exposed services, banners, certificates, and unmanaged assets

Reconnaissance.label: Technology fingerprinting
Reconnaissance.description: Identify web technologies, frameworks, servers, CDN/WAF providers, SaaS platforms, and exposed management panels

Reconnaissance.label: Cloud asset discovery
Reconnaissance.description: Search for exposed cloud storage, public buckets, cloud apps, tenant names, and provider-specific hostnames

Reconnaissance.label: Email and identity discovery
Reconnaissance.description: Collect email formats, usernames, exposed accounts, SSO portals, and identity provider hints

Reconnaissance.label: Social media / LinkedIn research
Reconnaissance.description: Identify employees, roles, technologies, naming conventions, and likely phishing or password-spray targets

Reconnaissance.label: GitHub / code repository search
Reconnaissance.description: Look for leaked credentials, internal hostnames, API keys, deployment scripts, and sensitive project references

Reconnaissance.label: Breach and credential exposure review
Reconnaissance.description: Check authorized sources for leaked credentials, password patterns, and reused corporate usernames

Reconnaissance.label: Prioritize attack surface
Reconnaissance.description: Rank discovered assets by exposure, sensitivity, technology risk, and likely business impact

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Host discovery / ping sweep
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Identify live hosts in scope using ICMP, TCP, UDP, and protocol-specific discovery where appropriate

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Full TCP port scan
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Scan all 65535 TCP ports on targets and preserve raw output for evidence

Scanning and Enumeration.label: UDP port scan (top ports)
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Scan common UDP ports such as DNS, SNMP, NTP, TFTP, IKE, and RPC services

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Service version detection
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Identify service versions, banners, TLS certificates, and protocol metadata on open ports

Scanning and Enumeration.label: TLS/SSL configuration review
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Check certificate validity, protocol support, weak ciphers, HSTS, and hostname mismatches

Scanning and Enumeration.label: DNS service enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Check for zone transfers, recursion, DNSSEC issues, wildcard records, and internal record leakage

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Web application fingerprinting
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Identify HTTP services, frameworks, CMS platforms, auth flows, exposed admin panels, and interesting headers

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Web crawling and content discovery
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Spider applications and brute-force common directories, files, backups, API paths, and hidden endpoints

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Virtual host enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Discover name-based virtual hosts and environment-specific hostnames on shared IPs

Scanning and Enumeration.label: API enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Identify REST, GraphQL, SOAP, Swagger/OpenAPI docs, API versions, auth schemes, and unauthenticated endpoints

Scanning and Enumeration.label: SSH enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Review SSH versions, supported algorithms, auth methods, banner disclosure, and weak configuration

Scanning and Enumeration.label: RDP / WinRM / remote admin enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Identify exposed remote administration services, NLA requirements, certificate details, and access controls

Scanning and Enumeration.label: SMB / NetBIOS enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Enumerate shares, signing requirements, null sessions, users, groups, policies, and exposed files

Scanning and Enumeration.label: LDAP / Kerberos / Active Directory enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Enumerate domain controllers, naming contexts, users, groups, SPNs, password policy, and AD attack paths where authorized

Scanning and Enumeration.label: FTP / TFTP / NFS enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Check for anonymous access, writable shares, exported filesystems, sensitive files, and insecure transfer services

Scanning and Enumeration.label: SNMP enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Test common community strings and extract system, network, user, and configuration details if available

Scanning and Enumeration.label: SMTP / mail service enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Check mail banners, relay behavior, user enumeration, STARTTLS, SPF, DKIM, and DMARC posture

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Database service enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Identify exposed MSSQL, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, Redis, MongoDB, Elasticsearch, and other data stores

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Cloud and container surface enumeration
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Look for exposed cloud consoles, metadata paths, Kubernetes APIs, container registries, dashboards, and storage endpoints

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Default credential check
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Test for default, vendor, and weak credentials on services where permitted by the rules of engagement

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Vulnerability scanning
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Run appropriate scanners such as nuclei, nikto, nmap scripts, and authenticated scanners where authorized

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Import scan results
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Import nmap XML and other tool outputs into the engagement record

Scanning and Enumeration.label: Validate and triage scanner results
Scanning and Enumeration.description: Manually confirm high-value findings, remove false positives, and prioritize exploitable weaknesses

Exploitation.label: Confirm authorization before exploitation
Exploitation.description: Verify exploit attempts are in scope, safe for the target, and aligned to rules of engagement

Exploitation.label: Validate misconfigurations
Exploitation.description: Exploit high-impact misconfigurations such as anonymous access, weak ACLs, exposed consoles, and insecure defaults

Exploitation.label: Weak credential and password attack testing
Exploitation.description: Perform password spraying, default credential, reuse, and brute-force testing only where explicitly permitted

Exploitation.label: Authentication testing
Exploitation.description: Test login, MFA, password reset, session handling, account lockout, and alternate authentication paths

Exploitation.label: Authorization and access control testing
Exploitation.description: Test IDOR, horizontal privilege escalation, vertical privilege escalation, forced browsing, and role bypasses

Exploitation.label: File upload testing
Exploitation.description: Test upload functionality for unsafe file types, path control, content-type bypass, and server-side execution

Exploitation.label: XSS testing
Exploitation.description: Test for reflected, stored, and DOM-based cross-site scripting across user-controlled input

Exploitation.label: SQL injection testing
Exploitation.description: Test input fields, parameters, headers, and APIs for error-based, blind, time-based, and out-of-band SQL injection

Exploitation.label: SSRF testing
Exploitation.description: Test server-side request functionality for access to internal services, cloud metadata, and restricted networks

Exploitation.label: LFI/RFI and path traversal testing
Exploitation.description: Test file parameters for local file inclusion, remote file inclusion, path traversal, and sensitive file reads

Exploitation.label: Command injection testing
Exploitation.description: Test inputs passed to operating system commands for direct, blind, and out-of-band command execution

Exploitation.label: Deserialization and template injection testing
Exploitation.description: Test serialized objects and template inputs for code execution, object injection, and sandbox escape

Exploitation.label: XXE and XML parser testing
Exploitation.description: Test XML inputs for external entity expansion, local file disclosure, SSRF, and parser misconfiguration

Exploitation.label: Race condition and business logic testing
Exploitation.description: Test workflow bypasses, replay, rate limits, transaction manipulation, and time-of-check/time-of-use flaws

Exploitation.label: Exploit known CVEs
Exploitation.description: Search and attempt controlled exploitation for identified vulnerable versions and confirmed exposures

Exploitation.label: Network service exploitation
Exploitation.description: Exploit vulnerable network services using controlled proof-of-concept techniques and avoid unnecessary disruption

Exploitation.label: Password hash cracking
Exploitation.description: Crack captured hashes using authorized wordlists, rules, and safe handling of sensitive material

Exploitation.label: Document proof of impact
Exploitation.description: Capture reproducible evidence that demonstrates impact without unnecessary data access or service disruption

Post-Exploitation.label: Initial access validation
Post-Exploitation.description: Document account, host, privilege level, access path, timestamps, and constraints for each successful compromise

Post-Exploitation.label: Host and environment enumeration
Post-Exploitation.description: Enumerate OS, hostname, users, groups, network interfaces, routes, installed software, processes, and security tooling

Post-Exploitation.label: Privilege escalation
Post-Exploitation.description: Attempt to escalate to root, SYSTEM, local admin, domain admin, or other privileged roles where authorized

Post-Exploitation.label: Credential harvesting
Post-Exploitation.description: Extract and securely handle stored credentials, tokens, keys, browser secrets, and password hashes only as permitted

Post-Exploitation.label: Credential validation and reuse
Post-Exploitation.description: Validate captured credentials safely and identify reuse across hosts, applications, VPN, cloud, or domain services

Post-Exploitation.label: Internal network discovery
Post-Exploitation.description: Map reachable internal networks, routes, DNS, segmentation boundaries, and newly accessible services from compromised hosts

Post-Exploitation.label: Active Directory enumeration
Post-Exploitation.description: Run BloodHound or equivalent analysis and enumerate trusts, delegation, ACLs, Kerberoasting, AS-REP roasting, and high-value groups if applicable

Post-Exploitation.label: Lateral movement
Post-Exploitation.description: Pivot to other systems using obtained credentials, sessions, or access paths where explicitly authorized

Post-Exploitation.label: Cloud and identity impact review
Post-Exploitation.description: Assess cloud metadata access, IAM permissions, service principals, tokens, storage access, and tenant-wide impact where relevant

Post-Exploitation.label: Data access demonstration
Post-Exploitation.description: Document access to sensitive data using minimal sample evidence and avoid bulk exfiltration

Post-Exploitation.label: Security control validation
Post-Exploitation.description: Observe whether EDR, logging, alerting, MFA, network segmentation, and hardening controls detect or block activity

Post-Exploitation.label: Persistence documentation
Post-Exploitation.description: Document potential persistence mechanisms without installing persistence unless explicitly authorized

Post-Exploitation.label: Evidence collection
Post-Exploitation.description: Capture screenshots, command output, timestamps, affected assets, and proof for all material access

Post-Exploitation.label: Cleanup and restoration
Post-Exploitation.description: Remove test artifacts, temporary files, accounts, payloads, shells, and revert benign changes where possible

Reporting.label: Verify scope documentation
Reporting.description: Ensure all tested, excluded, inaccessible, and out-of-scope assets are accurately documented

Reporting.label: Triage and de-duplicate findings
Reporting.description: Merge duplicate issues, group related weaknesses, and separate root causes from symptoms

Reporting.label: Complete all finding entries
Reporting.description: Ensure all findings have title, description, impact, affected assets, evidence, remediation, and references

Reporting.label: Attach evidence to findings
Reporting.description: Link screenshots, commands, tool output, timestamps, and reproduction evidence to each finding

Reporting.label: Write reproduction steps
Reporting.description: Provide clear, repeatable technical steps that explain how each finding was validated

Reporting.label: Assign severity and CVSS scores
Reporting.description: Rate each finding using business impact, exploitability, environmental context, and an appropriate CVSS score

Reporting.label: Write business impact
Reporting.description: Explain realistic consequences in terms of data exposure, privilege, operational risk, compliance, and business process impact

Reporting.label: Write remediation guidance
Reporting.description: Provide practical fixes, compensating controls, ownership hints, and verification steps for remediation teams

Reporting.label: Map references and classifications
Reporting.description: Add relevant CVEs, CWEs, OWASP categories, MITRE ATT&CK techniques, vendor advisories, and supporting references

Reporting.label: Write executive summary
Reporting.description: Summarize objectives, scope, key risks, business impact, and overall security posture for leadership

Reporting.label: Document methodology and limitations
Reporting.description: Describe testing approach, tools, constraints, assumptions, credentials used, and areas not fully assessed

Reporting.label: Proofread report
Reporting.description: Review for technical accuracy, typos, consistency, evidence quality, and client-ready language

Reporting.label: Generate and export report
Reporting.description: Generate the final report from this tool and verify formatting before delivery

Reporting.label: Retest planning
Reporting.description: Identify findings recommended for retest and define evidence needed to confirm remediation

Reporting.label: Client debrief preparation
Reporting.description: Prepare talking points, risk themes, remediation priorities, and technical appendix material for the client meeting
